title: Coelogyne tokduanbanoyana
taxonomy: Coelogyne tokduanbanoyana is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is classified within the subtribe Coelogyninae, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. This species is native to the Himalayas and is known for its unique characteristics and growth habits. Coelogyne tokduanbanoyana is also classified as an epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants or surfaces, rather than in the ground. The classification of Coelogyne tokduanbanoyana reflects its evolutionary relationships and distinct features within the plant kingdom.
uses: Coelogyne tokduanbanoyana, also known as the Tok Duang Orchid, has been traditionally used in Thai folk medicine for its potential health benefits. However, there is limited scientific research available to confirm its medicinal uses. The plant has been reported to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which could be beneficial in treating various infections. Its leaves and roots are sometimes used in traditional remedies, although the exact preparation and dosages are not well-documented. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Coelogyne tokduanbanoyana.
